- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

sdrpi|[HexSDR](https://github.com/HexSDR/) SDR in Raspberry Pi size [Notes](kernel_boot/boards/sdrpi/notes.md)|**NO** need 39 zcu102_fmcs2|[Xilinx ZCU102 board](https://www.amd.com/en/corporate/university-program/aup-boards/rfsoc4x2.html)|Need 41 neptunesdr|Low cost Zynq 7020 + AD9361 board (Unofficial!)|**NO** need 42 LibreSDR|[Low cost Zynq 7020 + AD9361 board (Unofficial!)](https://github.com/pavelyazev/openwifi-libresdr)|**NO** need 43 44 ## Build FPGA 45 46 * Pre-conditions: 47 * Vivado 2022.2 with Vitis. You should have: your_Xilinx_install_directory/Vitis (NOT Vitis_HLS!) 48 * You can add Vitis by running "Xilinx Design Tools --> Add Design Tools for Devices 2022.2" from Xilinx program group/menu in your OS start menu, or Help menu of Vivado. 49 * Install the evaluation license of [Xilinx Viterbi Decoder](https://www.xilinx.com/products/intellectual-property/viterbi_decoder.html) into Vivado. 50 * Ubuntu 18/20/22 LTS release (We test in these OS. Other OS might also work.) 51 * Install required packages, such as `sudo apt install libtinfo5` 52 * For Ubuntu 24 LTS, the default libtinfo6 won't work. You need to install libtinfo5 manually. 53 ``` 54 wget http://be.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/pool/main/n/ncurses/libtinfo5_6.1-1ubuntu1.18.04.1_amd64.deb 55 sudo dpkg -i ./libtinfo5_6.1-1ubuntu1.18.04.1_amd64.deb 56 ``` 57 58 * Prepare Analgo Devices HDL library (only run once): 59 ``` 60 export XILINX_DIR=your_Xilinx_install_directory 61 (Example: export XILINX_DIR=/opt/Xilinx. The Xilinx directory should include sth like: Downloads, SDK, Vivado, xic) 62 ./prepare_adi_lib.sh $XILINX_DIR 63 ``` 64 * Prepare Analgo Devices specific ip (only run once for each board you have): 65 ``` 66 export BOARD_NAME=your_board_name 67 (Example: export BOARD_NAME=zc706_fmcs2) 68 ./prepare_adi_board_ip.sh $XILINX_DIR $BOARD_NAME 69 (Don't need to wait till the building end. When you see "Building ABCD project [...", you can stop it.) 70 ``` 71 * Get the openofdm_rx into ip directory (only run once after openofdm is udpated): 72 ``` 73 ./get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh 74 ``` 75 * Generate ip_repo for the top level FPGA project (will take a while): 76 ``` 77 cd openwifi-hw/boards/$BOARD_NAME/ 78 ../create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR 79 ``` 80 When encountering an issue with Vitis HLS "ERROR: '2xxxxxxxxx' is an invalid argument. Please specify an integer value", apply the patch as described here: https://support.xilinx.com/s/article/76960. 81 82 * In the Vivado 83 ``` 84 source ../openwifi.tcl 85 Click "Generate Bitstream" in the Vivado GUI. 86 (Will take a while) 87 File --> Export --> Export Hardware --> Next --> Include bitstream --> Next --> Next --> Finish 88 ``` 89 (This step is invoked automatically by previous create_ip_repo.sh) 90 * In Linux, store the FPGA files to a specific directory: 91 ``` 92 cd openwifi-hw/boards 93 ./sdk_update.sh $BOARD_NAME $OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR 94 ``` 95 Above command will store the FPGA img (.xsa .ltx) and the related git info into another directory $OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR that can be picked up by openwifi software building environment later on. Please check README of the openwifi repository. 96 97 ## Modify IP cores 98 99 IP core project files are in "ip/ip_name" directory. "ip_name" example: xpu, tx_intf, etc. To create the IP project and do necessary work (modification, simulation, etc.), go to the ip/ip_name directory, then: 100 ``` 101 ../create_vivado_proj.sh $XILINX_DIR ip_name.tcl 102 ``` 103 To apply your new/modified IP to the top level FPGA project, start from "../create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR" in the board directory (Build FPGA section) to integrate your modified IP to the board FPGA design. 104 105 If your IP modification is complicated and encounter error while running create_ip_repo.sh, you should check create_ip_repo.sh/ip_repo_gen.tcl/etc, understand and modify them accordingly (for example to include your new added files). 106 107 **Change the baseband clock:** 108 109 ![](./bb-clk.jpg) 110 111 By default, 100MHz baseband clock is used. You can change the baseband clock by changing the NUM_CLK_PER_US at the beginning of openwifi.tcl. Available options: 240/100MHz for zcu102; 100/200MHz for zc706 and adrv9361z7035; 100MHz for the rest. Then re-run openwifi.tcl to create the new FPGA project. 112 113 ## Simulate IP cores 114 115 * Create the ip core project in Vivado. To achieve this, you need to follow the "Modify IP cores" section to create the IP's Vivado project. 116 * Normally you should see the top level testbench (..._tb.v) of that ip core in the Vivado "Sources" window (take openofdm_rx as example): 117 118 Go to the openofdm_rx IP directory, then run: 119 ./create_vivado_proj.sh $XILINX_DIR openofdm_rx.tcl 120 Then in Vivado 121 Sources --> Simulation Sources --> sim_1 --> dot11_tb 122 * To run the simulation, click "Run Simulation" --> "Run Behavoiral Simulation" under the "SIMULATION" in the "PROJECT MANAGER" window. It will take quite long time for the 1st time run due to the sub-ip-core compiling. Fortunately the sub-ip-core compiling is a time consuming step that occurs only one time. 123 * When the previous step is finished, you should see a simulation window displays many variable names and waveforms. Now click the small triangle, which points to the right and has "Run All (F3)" hints, on top to start the simulation. 124 * Please check the ..._tb.v to see how do we use $fopen, $fscanf and $fwrite to read test vectors and save the variables for checking later. Of course you can also check everything in the waveform window. 125 * The openofdm_rx_pre_def.v also includes important definitions for the simulation. 126 * After you modify some design files, just click the small circle with arrow, which has "Relaunch Simulation" hints, on top to re-launch the simulation. 127 * You can always drag the signals you need from the "SIMULATION" --> "Scope" window to the waveform window, and relaunch the simulation to check those signals' waveform. An example: 128 129 SIMULATION --> Scope --> Name --> dot11_tb --> dot11_inst --> ofdm_decoder_inst --> viterbi_inst 130 131 ## Conditional compile by verilog macro 132 133 While working on a stand alone IP, the create_vivado_proj.sh could accept more arguments. Some arguments will be converted to verilog macro pre-defines into ip_name_pre_def.v, which can be included by IP source files to enable/disable some code blocks. Check more info by running create_vivado_proj.sh: 134 ``` 135 usage: 136 Need at least 2 arguments: $XILINX_DIR $TCL_FILENAME 137 More arguments (max 7) will be passed as arguments to the .tcl script to create ip_name_pre_def.v 138 Among these max 7 arguments: 139 - the 1st: BOARD_NAME (antsdr zc706_fmcs2 zed_fmcs2 zc702_fmcs2 adrv9361z7035 adrv9364z7020 zcu102_fmcs2) 140 - the 2nd: NUM_CLK_PER_US (for example: input 100 for 100MHz) 141 - the 3rd-7th: User pre defines (assume it is ABC) for conditional compiling. Will be `define IP_NAME_ABC in ip_name_pre_def.v 142 - the 3rd exception: in the case of openofdm_rx, it indicates SAMPLE_FILE for simulation. Can be changed later in openofdm_rx_pre_def.v 143 ``` 144 While working on the top level FPGA project, the same verilog macro pre-defines should also be specified when running create_ip_repo.sh if you want the IP to be conditional compiled in the same way when you working on it in stand alone mode (when the IP project is created by create_vivado_proj.sh). Check more info by running create_ip_repo.sh: 145 ``` 146 usage: 147 create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR 148 or 149 create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR $IP1_NAME $DEF1 $DEF2 ... $IP2_NAME $DEF1 ... 150 -IP_NAME: only xpu/tx_intf/rx_intf/openofdm_tx/openofdm_rx/side_ch are allowed 151 - DEFx: will be "`define IP_NAME_DEFx" in ip_name_pre_def.v for $IP_NAME 152 ``` 153 Example of enabling all ILA/DEBUG macros in all IPs: 154 ``` 155 ./create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR xpu ENABLE_DBG tx_intf ENABLE_DBG rx_intf ENABLE_DBG openofdm_tx ENABLE_DBG openofdm_rx ENABLE_DBG side_ch ENABLE_DBG 156 ``` 157 158 ## Migrate openwifi to new ADI release and Vivado 159 160 There are two possible ways to upgrade openwifi design to new ADI release and Vivado. 161 162 Method 1: Vivado auto upgrading 163 - Create the openwifi design in the old/current Vivado version 164 - Open the openwifi project in the new/target Vivado version 165 - Let Vivado do the upgrading 166 - After upgrading, the system.bd is the new bd file for the new/target Vivado version 167 - Export the openwifi project in the new/target Vivado as .tcl file 168 - Compare this new .tcl file with the original openwifi.tcl to find out what need to be changed 169 - You can check our commit on openwifi.tcl to find out what we have changed by the comparison 170 171 Method 2: Start from new Vivado and ADI HDL reference design, then add openwifi IP 172 - Create the openwifi design in the old/current Vivado version 173 - Open the openwifi design with the new/target Vivado version 174 - Use write_bd_tcl to write openwifi_ip Hierarchy to a .tcl 175 ``` 176 write_bd_tcl -hier_blks [get_bd_cells /hier_mig] ./mig_hierarchy.tcl 177 ``` 178 - Create/open the new (or your own) ADI HDL reference design with the new/target Vivado version, then: 179 ``` 180 source ./mig_hierarchy.tcl 181 create_hier_cell_hier_mig / my_new_hierarchy 182 ``` 183 - The openwifi_ip sub-block (Hierarchy) should appear in your new design with new Vivado version. 184 185 Main reference: Vivado Design Suite User Guide: Designing IP Subsystems Using IP Integrator (UG994). Main command in that UG: 186 ``` 187 write_bd_tcl -hier_blks [get_bd_cells /hier_mig] ./mig_hierarchy.tcl 188 source ./mig_hierarchy.tcl 189 create_hier_cell_hier_mig / my_new_hierarchy 190 ``` 191 192 ***Note: openwifi adds necessary modules/modifications on top of [Analog Devices HDL reference design](https://github.com/analogdevicesinc/hdl). For general issues, Analog Devices wiki pages would be helpful!*** 193 194 ***Notes: The 802.11 ofdm receiver is based on [openofdm project](https://github.com/jhshi/openofdm). You can find our improvements in our openofdm fork (dot11zynq branch) which is mapped to ip/openofdm_rx.*** 195

antsdr_e200|[MicroPhase](https://github.com/MicroPhase/) enhanced ADALM-PLUTO (smaller/cheaper) [Notes](kernel_boot/boards/antsdr_e200/README.md)|**NO** need sdrpi|[HexSDR](https://github.com/HexSDR/) SDR in Raspberry Pi size [Notes](kernel_boot/boards/sdrpi/notes.md)|**NO** need zcu102_fmcs2|[Xilinx ZCU102 board](https://www.xilinx.com/products/boards-and-kits/ek-u1-zcu102-g.html) + [FMCOMMS2/3/4](https://www.analog.com/en/design-center/evaluation-hardware-and-software/evaluation-boards-kits/eval-ad-fmcomms2.html)|Need rfsoc4x2|[RFSoC4x2 board](https://www.amd.com/en/corporate/university-program/aup-boards/rfsoc4x2.html)|Need neptunesdr|Low cost Zynq 7020 + AD9361 board (Unofficial!)|**NO** need LibreSDR|[Low cost Zynq 7020 + AD9361 board (Unofficial!)](https://github.com/pavelyazev/openwifi-libresdr)|**NO** need ## Build FPGA * Pre-conditions: * Vivado 2022.2 with Vitis. You should have: your_Xilinx_install_directory/Vitis (NOT Vitis_HLS!) * You can add Vitis by running "Xilinx Design Tools --> Add Design Tools for Devices 2022.2" from Xilinx program group/menu in your OS start menu, or Help menu of Vivado. * Install the evaluation license of [Xilinx Viterbi Decoder](https://www.xilinx.com/products/intellectual-property/viterbi_decoder.html) into Vivado. * Ubuntu 18/20/22 LTS release (We test in these OS. Other OS might also work.) * Install required packages, such as `sudo apt install libtinfo5` * For Ubuntu 24 LTS, the default libtinfo6 won't work. You need to install libtinfo5 manually. ``` wget http://be.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/pool/main/n/ncurses/libtinfo5_6.1-1ubuntu1.18.04.1_amd64.deb sudo dpkg -i ./libtinfo5_6.1-1ubuntu1.18.04.1_amd64.deb ``` * Prepare Analgo Devices HDL library (only run once): ``` export XILINX_DIR=your_Xilinx_install_directory (Example: export XILINX_DIR=/opt/Xilinx. The Xilinx directory should include sth like: Downloads, SDK, Vivado, xic) ./prepare_adi_lib.sh $XILINX_DIR ``` * Prepare Analgo Devices specific ip (only run once for each board you have): ``` export BOARD_NAME=your_board_name (Example: export BOARD_NAME=zc706_fmcs2) ./prepare_adi_board_ip.sh $XILINX_DIR $BOARD_NAME (Don't need to wait till the building end. When you see "Building ABCD project [...", you can stop it.) ``` * Get the openofdm_rx into ip directory (only run once after openofdm is udpated): ``` ./get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh ``` * Generate ip_repo for the top level FPGA project (will take a while): ``` cd openwifi-hw/boards/$BOARD_NAME/ ../create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR ``` When encountering an issue with Vitis HLS "ERROR: '2xxxxxxxxx' is an invalid argument. Please specify an integer value", apply the patch as described here: https://support.xilinx.com/s/article/76960. * In the Vivado ``` source ../openwifi.tcl Click "Generate Bitstream" in the Vivado GUI. (Will take a while) File --> Export --> Export Hardware --> Next --> Include bitstream --> Next --> Next --> Finish ``` (This step is invoked automatically by previous create_ip_repo.sh) * In Linux, store the FPGA files to a specific directory: ``` cd openwifi-hw/boards ./sdk_update.sh $BOARD_NAME $OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR ``` Above command will store the FPGA img (.xsa .ltx) and the related git info into another directory $OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR that can be picked up by openwifi software building environment later on. Please check README of the openwifi repository. ## Modify IP cores IP core project files are in "ip/ip_name" directory. "ip_name" example: xpu, tx_intf, etc. To create the IP project and do necessary work (modification, simulation, etc.), go to the ip/ip_name directory, then: ``` ../create_vivado_proj.sh $XILINX_DIR ip_name.tcl ``` To apply your new/modified IP to the top level FPGA project, start from "../create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR" in the board directory (Build FPGA section) to integrate your modified IP to the board FPGA design. If your IP modification is complicated and encounter error while running create_ip_repo.sh, you should check create_ip_repo.sh/ip_repo_gen.tcl/etc, understand and modify them accordingly (for example to include your new added files). **Change the baseband clock:** ![](./bb-clk.jpg) By default, 100MHz baseband clock is used. You can change the baseband clock by changing the NUM_CLK_PER_US at the beginning of openwifi.tcl. Available options: 240/100MHz for zcu102; 100/200MHz for zc706 and adrv9361z7035; 100MHz for the rest. Then re-run openwifi.tcl to create the new FPGA project. ## Simulate IP cores * Create the ip core project in Vivado. To achieve this, you need to follow the "Modify IP cores" section to create the IP's Vivado project. * Normally you should see the top level testbench (..._tb.v) of that ip core in the Vivado "Sources" window (take openofdm_rx as example): Go to the openofdm_rx IP directory, then run: ./create_vivado_proj.sh $XILINX_DIR openofdm_rx.tcl Then in Vivado Sources --> Simulation Sources --> sim_1 --> dot11_tb * To run the simulation, click "Run Simulation" --> "Run Behavoiral Simulation" under the "SIMULATION" in the "PROJECT MANAGER" window. It will take quite long time for the 1st time run due to the sub-ip-core compiling. Fortunately the sub-ip-core compiling is a time consuming step that occurs only one time. * When the previous step is finished, you should see a simulation window displays many variable names and waveforms. Now click the small triangle, which points to the right and has "Run All (F3)" hints, on top to start the simulation. * Please check the ..._tb.v to see how do we use $fopen, $fscanf and $fwrite to read test vectors and save the variables for checking later. Of course you can also check everything in the waveform window. * The openofdm_rx_pre_def.v also includes important definitions for the simulation. * After you modify some design files, just click the small circle with arrow, which has "Relaunch Simulation" hints, on top to re-launch the simulation. * You can always drag the signals you need from the "SIMULATION" --> "Scope" window to the waveform window, and relaunch the simulation to check those signals' waveform. An example: SIMULATION --> Scope --> Name --> dot11_tb --> dot11_inst --> ofdm_decoder_inst --> viterbi_inst ## Conditional compile by verilog macro While working on a stand alone IP, the create_vivado_proj.sh could accept more arguments. Some arguments will be converted to verilog macro pre-defines into ip_name_pre_def.v, which can be included by IP source files to enable/disable some code blocks. Check more info by running create_vivado_proj.sh: ``` usage: Need at least 2 arguments: $XILINX_DIR $TCL_FILENAME More arguments (max 7) will be passed as arguments to the .tcl script to create ip_name_pre_def.v Among these max 7 arguments: - the 1st: BOARD_NAME (antsdr zc706_fmcs2 zed_fmcs2 zc702_fmcs2 adrv9361z7035 adrv9364z7020 zcu102_fmcs2) - the 2nd: NUM_CLK_PER_US (for example: input 100 for 100MHz) - the 3rd-7th: User pre defines (assume it is ABC) for conditional compiling. Will be `define IP_NAME_ABC in ip_name_pre_def.v - the 3rd exception: in the case of openofdm_rx, it indicates SAMPLE_FILE for simulation. Can be changed later in openofdm_rx_pre_def.v ``` While working on the top level FPGA project, the same verilog macro pre-defines should also be specified when running create_ip_repo.sh if you want the IP to be conditional compiled in the same way when you working on it in stand alone mode (when the IP project is created by create_vivado_proj.sh). Check more info by running create_ip_repo.sh: ``` usage: create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR or create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR $IP1_NAME $DEF1 $DEF2 ... $IP2_NAME $DEF1 ... -IP_NAME: only xpu/tx_intf/rx_intf/openofdm_tx/openofdm_rx/side_ch are allowed - DEFx: will be "`define IP_NAME_DEFx" in ip_name_pre_def.v for $IP_NAME ``` Example of enabling all ILA/DEBUG macros in all IPs: ``` ./create_ip_repo.sh $XILINX_DIR xpu ENABLE_DBG tx_intf ENABLE_DBG rx_intf ENABLE_DBG openofdm_tx ENABLE_DBG openofdm_rx ENABLE_DBG side_ch ENABLE_DBG ``` ## Migrate openwifi to new ADI release and Vivado There are two possible ways to upgrade openwifi design to new ADI release and Vivado. Method 1: Vivado auto upgrading - Create the openwifi design in the old/current Vivado version - Open the openwifi project in the new/target Vivado version - Let Vivado do the upgrading - After upgrading, the system.bd is the new bd file for the new/target Vivado version - Export the openwifi project in the new/target Vivado as .tcl file - Compare this new .tcl file with the original openwifi.tcl to find out what need to be changed - You can check our commit on openwifi.tcl to find out what we have changed by the comparison Method 2: Start from new Vivado and ADI HDL reference design, then add openwifi IP - Create the openwifi design in the old/current Vivado version - Open the openwifi design with the new/target Vivado version - Use write_bd_tcl to write openwifi_ip Hierarchy to a .tcl ``` write_bd_tcl -hier_blks [get_bd_cells /hier_mig] ./mig_hierarchy.tcl ``` - Create/open the new (or your own) ADI HDL reference design with the new/target Vivado version, then: ``` source ./mig_hierarchy.tcl create_hier_cell_hier_mig / my_new_hierarchy ``` - The openwifi_ip sub-block (Hierarchy) should appear in your new design with new Vivado version. Main reference: Vivado Design Suite User Guide: Designing IP Subsystems Using IP Integrator (UG994). Main command in that UG: ``` write_bd_tcl -hier_blks [get_bd_cells /hier_mig] ./mig_hierarchy.tcl source ./mig_hierarchy.tcl create_hier_cell_hier_mig / my_new_hierarchy ``` ***Note: openwifi adds necessary modules/modifications on top of [Analog Devices HDL reference design](https://github.com/analogdevicesinc/hdl). For general issues, Analog Devices wiki pages would be helpful!*** ***Notes: The 802.11 ofdm receiver is based on [openofdm project](https://github.com/jhshi/openofdm). You can find our improvements in our openofdm fork (dot11zynq branch) which is mapped to ip/openofdm_rx.***

#!/bin/bash

# Author: Xianjun jiao
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2025 UGent
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

print_usage () {
  echo "usage:"
  echo "Need at least 2 arguments: \$XILINX_DIR \$TCL_FILENAME"
  echo "More arguments (max 7) will be passed as arguments to the .tcl script to create ip_name_pre_def.v"
  echo "Among these max 7 arguments:"
  echo "- the 1st:     BOARD_NAME (antsdr antsdr_e200 e310v2 sdrpi zc706_fmcs2 zed_fmcs2 zc702_fmcs2 adrv9361z7035 adrv9364z7020 zcu102_fmcs2 neptunesdr)"
  echo "- the 2nd:     NUM_CLK_PER_US (for example: input 100 for 100MHz)"
  echo "- the 3rd-7th: User pre defines (assume it is ABC) for conditional compiling. Will be \`define IP_NAME_ABC in ip_name_pre_def.v"
  echo "  - the 3rd exception: in the case of openofdm_rx, it indicates SAMPLE_FILE for simulation. Can be changed later in openofdm_rx_pre_def.v"
}

print_usage

if [ "$#" -lt 2 ]; then
    exit 1
fi

XILINX_DIR=$1
TCL_FILENAME=$2

echo XILINX_DIR $XILINX_DIR
echo TCL_FILENAME $TCL_FILENAME

if [ -d "$XILINX_DIR/Vivado" ]; then
    echo "$XILINX_DIR is found!"
else
    echo "$XILINX_DIR is not correct. Please check!"
    exit 1
fi

if [ -f "$TCL_FILENAME" ]; then
    echo "$TCL_FILENAME is found!"
else
    echo "$TCL_FILENAME does NOT exist. Please check!"
    exit 1
fi

source $XILINX_DIR/Vivado/2022.2/settings64.sh

ARG1=""
ARG2=""
ARG3=""
ARG4=""
ARG5=""
ARG6=""
ARG7=""

if [[ -n $3 ]]; then
    ARG1=$3
fi
if [[ -n $4 ]]; then
    ARG2=$4
fi
if [[ -n $5 ]]; then
    ARG3=$5
fi
if [[ -n $6 ]]; then
    ARG4=$6
fi
if [[ -n $7 ]]; then
    ARG5=$7
fi
if [[ -n $8 ]]; then
    ARG6=$8
fi
if [[ -n $9 ]]; then
    ARG7=$9
fi

set -x
vivado -source $TCL_FILENAME -tclargs $ARG1 $ARG2 $ARG3 $ARG4 $ARG5 $ARG6 $ARG7
set +x

// Xianjun jiao. putaoshu@msn.com; xianjun.jiao@imec.be;
`include "openwifi_hw_git_rev.v"
`include "xpu_pre_def.v"

`ifdef XPU_ENABLE_DBG
`define DEBUG_PREFIX (*mark_debug="true",DONT_TOUCH="TRUE"*)
`else
`define DEBUG_PREFIX
`endif

`timescale 1 ns / 1 ps

module xpu #
(
  parameter integer GPIO_STATUS_WIDTH = 8,
  parameter integer DELAY_CTL_WIDTH = 7,
  parameter integer RSSI_HALF_DB_WIDTH = 11,
  parameter integer IQ_RSSI_HALF_DB_WIDTH = 9,
  parameter integer C_S00_AXIS_TDATA_WIDTH	= 64,
  parameter integer IQ_DATA_WIDTH	= 16,
  parameter integer WIFI_TX_BRAM_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
  parameter integer C_S00_AXI_DATA_WIDTH	= 32,
  parameter integer C_S00_AXI_ADDR_WIDTH	= 8,
  parameter integer TSF_TIMER_WIDTH = 64, // according to 802.11 standard
  parameter integer WIFI_TX_BRAM_ADDR_WIDTH = 10
)
(
  // ad9361 status and ctrl
  input  wire [(GPIO_STATUS_WIDTH-1):0] gpio_status,

  // Ports to rx_intf
  input  wire signed [(IQ_DATA_WIDTH-1):0] ddc_i,
  input  wire signed [(IQ_DATA_WIDTH-1):0] ddc_q,
  input  wire ddc_iq_valid,
  output wire mute_adc_out_to_bb, // when tx, mute self rx
  output wire block_rx_dma_to_ps, // should valid from filter on to fcs valid
  output wire block_rx_dma_to_ps_valid, // should valid from filter on to fcs valid
  output wire signed [(RSSI_HALF_DB_WIDTH-1):0] rssi_half_db_lock_by_sig_valid,
  output wire [(GPIO_STATUS_WIDTH-1):0] gpio_status_lock_by_sig_valid,
  `DEBUG_PREFIX output wire [(TSF_TIMER_WIDTH-1):0]  tsf_runtime_val,
  `DEBUG_PREFIX output wire tsf_pulse_1M,
      
  // Ports to openofdm rx
  output wire signed [(RSSI_HALF_DB_WIDTH-1):0] rssi_half_db,
  input  wire demod_is_ongoing,
  //input  wire pkt_begin,
  //input  wire pkt_ht,
  input  wire pkt_header_valid,
  input  wire pkt_header_valid_strobe,
  input  wire ht_unsupport,
  input  wire [7:0] pkt_rate,
  input  wire [15:0] pkt_len,
  //input  wire [15:0] pkt_len_total, // for interface to byte_to_word.v in rx_intf.v
  input  wire byte_in_strobe,
  input  wire [7:0] byte_in,
  //input  wire [15:0] byte_count_total, // for interface to byte_to_word.v in rx_intf.v
  input  wire [15:0] byte_count,
  input  wire fcs_in_strobe,
  input  wire fcs_ok,
  // phy len info
  input [14:0] n_ofdm_sym,//max 20166 = (22+65535*8)/26 (max ht len 65535 in sig, min ndbps 26 for mcs0)
  input [9:0]  n_bit_in_last_sym,//max ht ndbps 260 (ht mcs7)
  input        phy_len_valid,

  input  wire rx_ht_aggr,
  input  wire rx_ht_aggr_last,

  // led (flip per event) & gpio (raw)
  output wire demod_is_ongoing_led,
  output wire cycle_start0_led,
  output wire phy_tx_started_led,
  output wire sig_valid_led,

  // Ports to phy_tx
  input  wire phy_tx_start,
  input  wire phy_tx_started,
  input  wire phy_tx_done,

  // Ports to tx_intf
  output wire [79:0] tx_status,
  output wire [47:0] mac_addr,
  output wire retrans_in_progress,
  `DEBUG_PREFIX output wire start_retrans,
  `DEBUG_PREFIX output wire start_tx_ack,
  output wire tx_try_complete,
  input  wire tx_iq_fifo_empty,
  output wire [3:0] slice_en,
  output wire backoff_done,
  output wire tx_bb_is_ongoing,
  output wire tx_rf_is_ongoing,
  output wire ack_tx_flag,
  output wire wea,
  output wire [9:0] addra,
  output wire [(C_S00_AXIS_TDATA_WIDTH-1):0] dina,
  input  wire tx_pkt_need_ack,
  input  wire [3:0] tx_pkt_retrans_limit,
  input  wire tx_ht_aggr,
  input  wire [(WIFI_TX_BRAM_DATA_WIDTH-1):0] douta,//from dpram of tx_intf, for tx_control changing some bits to indicate it is the 1st pkt or retransmitted pkt
  input  wire cts_toself_bb_is_ongoing,//this should rise before the phy tx end valid of phy tx IP core to avoid tx_control waiting ack for this tx
  input  wire cts_toself_rf_is_ongoing,//just need to cover the SIFS gap between cts tx and following packet tx
  input wire  [(WIFI_TX_BRAM_ADDR_WIDTH-1):0] bram_addr,
  output wire [3:0] band,
  output wire [15:0] channel,
  input wire quit_retrans,
  input wire reset_backoff,
  output wire [3:0] tx_control_state,
  output wire tx_control_state_idle,
  output wire [9:0] num_slot_random,
  output wire [3:0] cw,
  input wire high_trigger,
  input wire [1:0] tx_queue_idx,

  // to side channel
  output wire [31:0] FC_DI,
  output wire FC_DI_valid,
  output wire [47:0] addr1,
  output wire addr1_valid,
  output wire [47:0] addr2,
  output wire addr2_valid,
  output wire [47:0] addr3,

60	  puts "\nDescription:"
61	  puts "Recreate a Vivado project from this script. The created project will be"
62	  puts "functionally equivalent to the original project for which this script was"
63	  puts "generated. The script contains commands for creating a project, filesets,"
64	  puts "runs, adding/importing sources and setting properties on various objects.\n"
65	  puts "Syntax:"

67	  puts "$script_file -tclargs \[--origin_dir <path>\]"

85	if { $::argc > 0 } {

# // Author: Xianjun Jiao
# // SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2025 UGent
# // SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

# common Vivado top project script for all boards

# https://adaptivesupport.amd.com/s/article/000034290?language=en_US
set_param gui.addressMap 0

# Set the reference directory for source file relative paths (by default the value is script directory path)
set origin_dir "."

source ./set_files.tcl

# Set board specific variables
set BOARD_NAME [lindex [split [exec pwd] /] end]
puts "openwifi.tcl BOARD_NAME $BOARD_NAME"
source ../../ip/parse_board_name.tcl

# This overrides the value in ip_repo_gen.tcl!
set NUM_CLK_PER_US 100
set  fd  [open  "./ip_repo/clock_speed.v"  w]
puts $fd "`define NUM_CLK_PER_US $NUM_CLK_PER_US"
if {$fpga_size_flag == 0} {
  puts $fd "`define SMALL_FPGA 1"
}
close $fd
exec cp ./ip_repo/clock_speed.v ./ip_repo/tx_intf/src/ -f
exec cp ./ip_repo/clock_speed.v ./ip_repo/rx_intf/src/ -f
exec cp ./ip_repo/clock_speed.v ./ip_repo/xpu/src/ -f

# -----------generate git rev info (overwrite ip_repo_gen.tcl)---
set  fd  [open  "./ip_repo/xpu/src/openwifi_hw_git_rev.v"  w]
set HASHCODE [exec ../../get_git_rev.sh]
puts $fd "`define OPENWIFI_HW_GIT_REV (32'h$HASHCODE)"
close $fd
# ----end of generate generate git rev info----------------------

# Use origin directory path location variable, if specified in the tcl shell
if { [info exists ::origin_dir_loc] } {
  set origin_dir $::origin_dir_loc
}

# Set the project name
set _xil_proj_name_ "openwifi_$BOARD_NAME"
exec rm -rf $_xil_proj_name_
exec git clean -dxf ./src/

# Use project name variable, if specified in the tcl shell
if { [info exists ::user_project_name] } {
  set _xil_proj_name_ $::user_project_name
}

variable script_file
set script_file "openwifi.tcl"

# Help information for this script
proc print_help {} {
  variable script_file
  puts "\nDescription:"
  puts "Recreate a Vivado project from this script. The created project will be"
  puts "functionally equivalent to the original project for which this script was"
  puts "generated. The script contains commands for creating a project, filesets,"
  puts "runs, adding/importing sources and setting properties on various objects.\n"
  puts "Syntax:"
  puts "$script_file"
  puts "$script_file -tclargs \[--origin_dir <path>\]"
  puts "$script_file -tclargs \[--project_name <name>\]"
  puts "$script_file -tclargs \[--help\]\n"
  puts "Usage:"
  puts "Name                   Description"
  puts "-------------------------------------------------------------------------"
  puts "\[--origin_dir <path>\]  Determine source file paths wrt this path. Default"
  puts "                       origin_dir path value is \".\", otherwise, the value"
  puts "                       that was set with the \"-paths_relative_to\" switch"
  puts "                       when this script was generated.\n"
  puts "\[--project_name <name>\] Create project with the specified name. Default"
  puts "                       name is the name of the project from where this"
  puts "                       script was generated.\n"
  puts "\[--help\]               Print help information for this script"
  puts "-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n"
  exit 0
}

if { $::argc > 0 } {
  for {set i 0} {$i < $::argc} {incr i} {
    set option [string trim [lindex $::argv $i]]
    switch -regexp -- $option {
      "--origin_dir"   { incr i; set origin_dir [lindex $::argv $i] }
      "--project_name" { incr i; set _xil_proj_name_ [lindex $::argv $i] }
      "--help"         { print_help }
      default {
        if { [regexp {^-} $option] } {
          puts "ERROR: Unknown option '$option' specified, please type '$script_file -tclargs --help' for usage info.\n"
          return 1
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

# Set the directory path for the original project from where this script was exported
set orig_proj_dir "[file normalize "$origin_dir/"]"

# Create project
create_project ${_xil_proj_name_} ./${_xil_proj_name_} -part $part_string

# Set the directory path for the new project
set proj_dir [get_property directory [current_project]]

# Set project properties
set obj [current_project]
set_property -name "board_part_repo_paths" -value "$board_part_repos" -objects $obj
set_property -name "board_part" -value "$board_part_string" -objects $obj
set_property -name "classic_soc_boot" -value "0" -objects $obj
set_property -name "compxlib.activehdl_compiled_library_dir" -value "$proj_dir/${_xil_proj_name_}.cache/compile_simlib/activehdl" -objects $obj
set_property -name "compxlib.funcsim" -value "1" -objects $obj

set_property -name "compxlib.riviera_compiled_library_dir" -value "$proj_dir/${_xil_proj_name_}.cache/compile_simlib/riviera" -objects $obj
set_property -name "compxlib.timesim" -value "1" -objects $obj
set_property -name "compxlib.vcs_compiled_library_dir" -value "$proj_dir/${_xil_proj_name_}.cache/compile_simlib/vcs" -objects $obj
set_property -name "compxlib.xsim_compiled_library_dir" -value "" -objects $obj

set_property -name "enable_optional_runs_sta" -value "0" -objects $obj
set_property -name "enable_vhdl_2008" -value "1" -objects $obj
set_property -name "generate_ip_upgrade_log" -value "1" -objects $obj

#!/bin/bash

# Author: Xianjun jiao
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2022 UGent
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

print_usage () {
  echo "usage:"
  echo "create_ip_repo.sh \$XILINX_DIR"
  echo "or"
  echo "create_ip_repo.sh \$XILINX_DIR \$IP1_NAME \$DEF1 \$DEF2 ... \$IP2_NAME \$DEF1 ..."
  echo " -IP_NAME: only xpu/tx_intf/rx_intf/openofdm_tx/openofdm_rx/side_ch are allowed"
  echo " -   DEFx: will be \"\`define IP_NAME_DEFx\" in ip_name_pre_def.v for \$IP_NAME"
  echo " "
}

print_usage

if [ "$#" -lt 1 ]; then
    exit 1
fi

XILINX_DIR=$1

start_to_write=0
mkdir -p ip_config
rm -rf ip_config/*

BOARD_NAME=${PWD##*/}
echo $BOARD_NAME

XILINX_ENV_FILE=$XILINX_DIR/Vitis/2022.2/settings64.sh
echo "Expect env file $XILINX_ENV_FILE"

if [ -f "$XILINX_ENV_FILE" ]; then
    echo "$XILINX_ENV_FILE is found!"
else
    echo "$XILINX_ENV_FILE is not correct. Please check!"
    exit 1
fi

IP_NAME_ALL="xpu tx_intf rx_intf openofdm_tx openofdm_rx side_ch"
for IP_NAME in $IP_NAME_ALL
do
    filename_to_write=ip_config/$IP_NAME"_pre_def.v"
    echo "//Naming pre_def.v differently for all IPs." > $filename_to_write
    echo "//Multiple pre_def.v with different content for different IP are not allowed in the final signle Vivado project!" >> $filename_to_write
    echo "\`define $BOARD_NAME" >> $filename_to_write
done

MODULE_NAME=""
for ARGUMENT in "$@"
do
    if [ "$ARGUMENT" = "xpu" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "tx_intf" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "rx_intf" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "openofdm_tx" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "openofdm_rx" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "side_ch" ]; then
        start_to_write=1
    fi

    if [ $start_to_write == "1" ]; then
        if [ "$ARGUMENT" = "xpu" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "tx_intf" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "rx_intf" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "openofdm_tx" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "openofdm_rx" ] || [ "$ARGUMENT" = "side_ch" ]; then
            filename_to_write=ip_config/$ARGUMENT"_pre_def.v"
            echo "" >> $filename_to_write
            echo "//Pre defines for IP $ARGUMENT. Please align with those when you design/customize/modify the IP" >> $filename_to_write
            
            echo ""
            MODULE_NAME=${ARGUMENT^^}
            echo "$MODULE_NAME:"
        else
            echo "\`define ${MODULE_NAME}_${ARGUMENT}" >> $filename_to_write

            echo "\`define ${MODULE_NAME}_${ARGUMENT}"
        fi
    fi
done

source $XILINX_ENV_FILE

set -x
vivado -source ../ip_repo_gen.tcl
set +x

################################################################
# This is a generated script based on design: system
#
# Though there are limitations about the generated script,
# the main purpose of this utility is to make learning
# IP Integrator Tcl commands easier.
################################################################

namespace eval _tcl {
proc get_script_folder {} {
   set script_path [file normalize [info script]]
   set script_folder [file dirname $script_path]
   return $script_folder
}
}
variable script_folder
set script_folder [_tcl::get_script_folder]

################################################################
# Check if script is running in correct Vivado version.
################################################################
set scripts_vivado_version 2018.3
set current_vivado_version [version -short]

if { [string first $scripts_vivado_version $current_vivado_version] == -1 } {
   puts ""
   catch {common::send_msg_id "BD_TCL-109" "ERROR" "This script was generated using Vivado <$scripts_vivado_version> and is being run in <$current_vivado_version> of Vivado. Please run the script in Vivado <$scripts_vivado_version> then open the design in Vivado <$current_vivado_version>. Upgrade the design by running \"Tools => Report => Report IP Status...\", then run write_bd_tcl to create an updated script."}

   return 1
}

################################################################
# START
################################################################

# To test this script, run the following commands from Vivado Tcl console:
# source system_script.tcl

set bCheckIPsPassed 1
##################################################################
# CHECK IPs
##################################################################
set bCheckIPs 1
if { $bCheckIPs == 1 } {
   set list_check_ips "\ 
xilinx.com:ip:axi_dma:7.1\
user.org:user:openofdm_rx:1.0\
user.org:user:openofdm_tx:1.0\
user.org:user:rx_intf:1.0\
xilinx.com:ip:proc_sys_reset:5.0\
user.org:user:tx_intf:1.0\
xilinx.com:ip:xlslice:1.0\
user.org:user:xpu:1.0\
"

   set list_ips_missing ""
   common::send_msg_id "BD_TCL-006" "INFO" "Checking if the following IPs exist in the project's IP catalog: $list_check_ips ."

   foreach ip_vlnv $list_check_ips {
      set ip_obj [get_ipdefs -all $ip_vlnv]
      if { $ip_obj eq "" } {
         lappend list_ips_missing $ip_vlnv
      }
   }

   if { $list_ips_missing ne "" } {
      catch {common::send_msg_id "BD_TCL-115" "ERROR" "The following IPs are not found in the IP Catalog:\n  $list_ips_missing\n\nResolution: Please add the repository containing the IP(s) to the project." }
      set bCheckIPsPassed 0
   }

}

if { $bCheckIPsPassed != 1 } {
  common::send_msg_id "BD_TCL-1003" "WARNING" "Will not continue with creation of design due to the error(s) above."
  return 3
}

##################################################################
# DESIGN PROCs

BOARD_NAME=$2

# [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "zc706_fmcs2" ] && [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "zc702_fmcs2" ] && [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "zed_fmcs2" ] && [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "adrv9361z7035" ] && [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "adrv9361z7035_fmc" ] && [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "adrv9364z7020" ] && [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "zcu102_fmcs2" ] && [ "$BOARD_NAME" != "zcu102_9371" ]; then

if [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "zcu102_fmcs2" ]; then
    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/fmcomms2/zcu102/
elif [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "zcu102_9371" ]; then
    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/fmcomms2/zcu102/
elif [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "zc706_fmcs2" ]; then
    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/fmcomms2/zc706/
elif [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "zc702_fmcs2" ]; then
    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/fmcomms2/zc702/
elif [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "zed_fmcs2" ]; then
    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/fmcomms2/zed/
elif [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "adrv9361z7035" ]; then
    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/adrv9361z7035/ccbob_lvds/
#elif [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "adrv9361z7035_fmc" ]; then
#    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/adrv9361z7035/ccfmc_lvds/
elif [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "adrv9364z7020" ] || [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "antsdr" ] ||  [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "antsdr_e200" ] || [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "e310v2" ] || [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "sdrpi" ] || [ "$BOARD_NAME" == "neptunesdr" ]; then
    ADI_PROJECT_DIR=./adi-hdl/projects/adrv9364z7020/ccbob_lvds/

    echo "\$BOARD_NAME is not correct. Please check!"

home_dir=$(pwd)

set -x

source $XILINX_DIR/Vivado/2022.2/settings64.sh

cd $ADI_PROJECT_DIR
make

cd $home_dir

#!/bin/bash

# // Author: Xianjun Jiao
# // SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2022 UGent
# // SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

if [ "$#" -ne 2 ]; then
    echo "You must enter the \$BOARD_NAME and \$OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR as argument"
    echo "Like: antsdr antsdr_e200 sdrpi e310v2 adrv9364z7020 adrv9361z7035 zc706_fmcs2 zed_fmcs2 zc702_fmcs2 zcu102_fmcs2 zcu102_9371 neptunesdr"
    exit 1
fi

set -ex

BOARD_NAME=$1
OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR=$2
echo $BOARD_NAME
echo $OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR

TARGET_SDK_DIR=$OPENWIFI_HW_IMG_DIR/boards/$BOARD_NAME/sdk/
if test -d "$TARGET_SDK_DIR"; then
    echo "Found $TARGET_SDK_DIR"
else
    echo "Create $TARGET_SDK_DIR"
    mkdir $TARGET_SDK_DIR -p
fi

rm -rf $TARGET_SDK_DIR/*
cp $BOARD_NAME/openwifi_$BOARD_NAME/system_top.xsa $TARGET_SDK_DIR/ -rf
if [ -f "$BOARD_NAME/openwifi_$BOARD_NAME/openwifi_$BOARD_NAME.runs/impl_1/system_top.ltx" ]; then
    cp $BOARD_NAME/openwifi_$BOARD_NAME/openwifi_$BOARD_NAME.runs/impl_1/system_top.ltx $TARGET_SDK_DIR/ -rf
else
    echo "No debug probe file found."
fi

echo "openwifi-hw-git-branch" >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
git branch >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
echo " " >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
echo "openwifi-hw-git-commit" >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
git log -3 >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
echo " " >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
echo "openofdm_rx-git-branch" >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
git --git-dir ../ip/openofdm_rx/.git branch >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
echo " " >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
echo "openofdm_rx-git-commit" >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
git --git-dir ../ip/openofdm_rx/.git log -3 >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt
echo " " >> $TARGET_SDK_DIR//git_info.txt

# https://adaptivesupport.amd.com/s/article/000034290?language=en_US
set_param gui.addressMap 0

# Set board specific variables
set BOARD_NAME [lindex [split [exec pwd] /] end]
puts "ip_repo_gen.tcl BOARD_NAME $BOARD_NAME"
source ../../ip/parse_board_name.tcl

# ------------------setup ip_repo directory and board files---------------------
exec rm -rf ip_repo

puts $fd "`define OPENWIFI_HW_GIT_REV (32'h$HASHCODE)"
close $fd
# ----end of generate generate git rev info---------------

# -----------generate has_side_ch_flag.v------------------
# if you want NO side_ch, please use set has_side_ch 0
set has_side_ch 1 
set  fd  [open  "./ip_repo/has_side_ch_flag.v"  w]
if {$has_side_ch > 0} {
  puts $fd "`define HAS_SIDE_CH 1"
} else {
  puts $fd "`define NO_SIDE_CH 1"
}
close $fd
# ----end of generate has_side_ch_flag.v------------------

# ---------generate fpga_scale.v---------------------------
set  fd  [open  "./ip_repo/fpga_scale.v"  w]
if {$fpga_size_flag == 0} {
  puts $fd "`define SIDE_CH_LESS_BRAM 1"
}
close $fd

  puts $fd "`define SMALL_FPGA 1"
}
close $fd
# --end of generate clock_speed.v for xpu/tx_intf/rx_intf--

# ---------generate spi_command.v---------------------------
# set grounded_rf_port 1 for port control, set 0 for lo control
set grounded_rf_port 0
set  fd  [open  "./ip_repo/spi_command.v"  w]
if {$grounded_rf_port == 1} {
  puts $fd "`define SPI_HIGH 24'hC22001"
  puts $fd "`define SPI_LOW 24'hC02001"
} else {
  puts $fd "`define SPI_HIGH 24'h088A01"
  puts $fd "`define SPI_LOW 24'h008A01"
}
close $fd
# ---------end of generate spi_command.v--------------------

}

I now have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the openwifi-hw project. Let me summarize my understanding:

## Project Overview
**openwifi-hw** is the Hardware/FPGA repository of the OpenWifi project — a Linux mac80211 compatible full-stack IEEE 802.11/Wi-Fi design based on SDR (Software Defined Radio). It runs on Xilinx Zynq FPGAs paired with Analog Devices AD9361/AD9364 RF transceivers (FMCOMMS2/3/4, ADRV9361, etc.).

## Architecture
The design is built on top of the Analog Devices HDL reference design. OpenWifi adds several custom IP cores that implement the WiFi PHY and lower MAC. The PS (Processing System - ARM cores in Zynq) runs Linux + mac80211 + the openwifi driver. The PL (Programmable Logic - FPGA) implements:

The custom openwifi IP cores:
1. **xpu** (eXternal Processing Unit) - The "brain"/control core: CSMA/CA, TX control (retransmission/ACK), TSF timer, packet filtering (addr1/addr2/addr3 parse), RSSI, CCA, SPI control to AD9361, slice_en (queues)
   - Submodules: csma_ca.v, tx_control.v, phy_rx_parse.v, tsf_timer.v, cca.v, rssi.v, spi.v, time_slice_gen.v, dc_rm.v, mv_avg.v, etc.
2. **tx_intf** - TX interface: connects to DAC DMA, holds packet in BRAM, manages TX bit/IQ interface, HT SIG CRC, CSI fuzzer, TX interrupt
   - Submodules: tx_bit_intf.v, tx_iq_intf.v, dac_intf.v, tx_status_fifo.v, csi_fuzzer.v, ht_sig_crc_calc.v, etc.
3. **rx_intf** - RX interface: connects to ADC, packet DMA to PS, FCS, byte-to-word, mute during TX, captures IQ for side channel
   - Submodules: adc_intf.v, rx_iq_intf.v, rx_intf_m_axis.v, byte_to_word_fcs_sn_insert.v, etc.
4. **openofdm_tx** - TX OFDM modulator: convolutional encoder, interleaver, modulation, IFFT, preamble ROMs (L-STF/L-LTF/HT-STF/HT-LTF), CRC32, scrambling
   - Top: openofdm_tx.v with dot11_tx.v, ifftmain.v, modulation.v, convenc.v, punc_interlv_lut.v, crc32_tx.v, *_rom.v
5. **openofdm_rx** - RX OFDM demodulator (submodule from openofdm/openofdm fork, dot11zynq branch) - sync, FFT, channel equalization, Viterbi decode
6. **side_ch** - Side channel: captures CSI, RSSI, TX/RX IQ samples, counter events for research/debug/monitoring
   - Submodules: side_ch_control.v, side_ch_counter.v, side_ch_m_axis.v, dpram.v

## Build System
- Vivado 2022.2 + Vitis, TCL-driven
- `prepare_adi_lib.sh` - prepare ADI HDL library (once)
- `prepare_adi_board_ip.sh` - prepare ADI board-specific IP (per board)
- `get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh` - fetch openofdm_rx submodule
- `boards/create_ip_repo.sh` → `ip_repo_gen.tcl` → `package_ip_complex.tcl` → `openwifi.tcl` (build flow)
- `boards/$BOARD_NAME/` - per-board configs (system.bd block design, system.xdc constraints, system_top.v wrapper)
- `ip/openwifi_ip.tcl` - creates the openwifi_ip block design hierarchy connecting xpu/tx_intf/rx_intf/openofdm_tx/openofdm_rx/side_ch
- `ip/connect_openwifi_ip.tcl` - connects openwifi_ip to PS (AXI, interrupts, DMA)
- Conditional compile via verilog macros (`_pre_def.v`, generated by create_ip_repo.sh/create_vivado_proj.sh)
- `sdk_update.sh` - exports .xsa/.ltx + git info to image dir

## Key concepts
- `board_def.v` - NUM_CLK_PER_US, SAMPLING_RATE_MHZ (20), sample/clock math
- Conditional compile macros: SMALL_FPGA, HAS_SIDE_CH, SIDE_CH_LESS_BRAM, ENABLE_DBG
- AXI4-Lite register interfaces (s_axi) for each IP, AXI4-Stream (axis) for data, AXI DMA for PS<->FPGA packet transfer
- Interrupts: tx_itrpt0/1, rx_pkt_intr, mm2s/s2mm_introut

content.text:
我已经彻底理解了该项目。openwifi-hw 仓库是基于 Xilinx Zynq + Analog Devices AD9361/9364 射频收发机的 IEEE 802.11/Wi-Fi 设计的 FPGA/硬件部分。它构建在 ADI HDL 参考设计之上，并增加了 6 个自定义 WiFi IP 核心：**xpu**（CSMA/CA、重传、TSF、数据包过滤、RSSI/CCA/SPI 的中央控制器）、**tx_intf**（TX BRAM/DMA/DAC）、**rx_intf**（ADC/DMA/FCS）、**openofdm_tx**（OFDM 调制器）、**openofdm_rx**（外部 OFDM 解调器）和 **side_ch**（监控/CSI 捕获）。构建过程完全由 TCL 驱动（使用 Vivado 2022.2）。

让我来生成 manifest。

input.head: d047d794195beb72e12d2a9a6c205c16399cf288
input.rationale: openwifi-hw 是一个基于 Xilinx Zynq SoC + Analog Devices AD9361/9364 射频前端的 IEEE 802.11 a/b/g/n 全栈 SDR Wi-Fi FPGA 设计，构建在 ADI HDL 参考设计之上。学习顺序按“先看清全貌、再沿数据链路深入、最后做二次开发”设计：u1 让读者从零建立项目定位、目录、板卡与构建流程的整体认知；u2 进入顶层 block design 与 PS-PL 互连，理解 6 个自定义 IP（xpu/tx_intf/rx_intf/openofdm_tx/openofdm_rx/side_ch）如何拼接成 openwifi_ip 层级并与 PS 通信；u3-u4 沿接收与发射两条物理层数据链路逐模块读源码；u5 聚焦控制核心 xpu 与低层 MAC（CSMA/CA、重传/ACK、TSF、包解析过滤、CCA/RSSI/SPI）；u6 讲可观测性的 side_ch；u7 面向专家层的二次开发（寄存器映射、条件编译宏、仿真 testbench、自定义 IP 打包、板卡移植、GPIO/ILA 调试）。每篇讲义只解决一个主题并绑定真实源码与实践任务，依赖关系沿“总览→链路→控制→开发”递进。所有讲义均为新增（action=new）。

lectures.title: 项目总览：什么是 openwifi 与 SDR Wi-Fi
lectures.topic: 介绍 openwifi 项目定位：Linux mac80211 兼容的 SDR 全栈 Wi-Fi 设计，说明 openwifi-hw（FPGA 硬件）与 openwifi（驱动/软件）仓库的关系，以及 AGPLv3 与订阅双授权模式。
learning_goals: 理解 openwifi 是基于 SDR 的 IEEE 802.11 全栈实现
区分 openwifi-hw 硬件仓库与 openwifi 软件仓库的职责
了解项目授权与第三方模块（ADI HDL、openofdm）来源
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README.md，用自己的话写出 openwifi-hw 在整套 openwifi 系统中承担的角色，并列举它依赖的两个第三方模块（adi-hdl、openofdm）的作用。

lectures.title: 目录结构、子模块与软硬件边界
lectures.topic: 讲解仓库顶层目录布局：ip/（自定义 WiFi IP 核心）、boards/（板卡工程）、adi-hdl（ADI 参考设计子模块）、ip/openofdm_rx（openofdm 子模块），以及 .gitmodules 子模块配置。
learning_goals: 掌握仓库主要目录的功能划分
理解 adi-hdl 与 openofdm_rx 两个 git 子模块的作用
区分 FPGA 侧（PL）与 ARM 侧（PS）的代码边界
lectures.practice_task: 画出仓库的目录树，标注 ip/ 下 6 个自定义 IP 核心目录，并用 git submodule status（或阅读 .gitmodules）确认 adi-hdl 与 openofdm_rx 两个子模块。

lectures.title: 支持的板卡与开发运行环境
lectures.topic: 介绍 README 中列出的全部 BOARD_NAME 板卡（zc706/zed/zc702/adrv9361z7035/adrv9364z7020/zcu102/antsdr/e310v2/sdrpi 等）及其 Vivado 许可需求，以及 Vivado 2022.2 + Vitis、libtinfo5 等运行前置条件。
learning_goals: 了解 openwifi-hw 支持的硬件板卡与射频前端组合
理解 BOARD_NAME 环境变量如何驱动构建
掌握 Vivado/Vitis 版本与系统依赖要求
lectures.practice_task: 在 README 的板卡表中任选一块板卡，整理出它对应的 FPGA 芯片、射频前端、是否需要 Vivado 许可，并列出构建该板卡所需的环境变量。

lectures.title: FPGA 构建全流程实战（脚本链路）
lectures.topic: 从 prepare_adi_lib.sh、prepare_adi_board_ip.sh、get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh，到 boards/create_ip_repo.sh → ip_repo_gen.tcl → openwifi.tcl，再到 sdk_update.sh，梳理完整的“准备依赖-生成 IP 仓-建顶层工程-导出镜像”脚本链路。
learning_goals: 掌握 openwifi-hw 从零到 bitstream 的完整脚本流程
理解 create_ip_repo.sh 与 openwifi.tcl 各自的职责
了解 .xsa/.ltx 镜像如何导出供软件仓库使用
lectures.practice_task: 按照 README 的 Build FPGA 小节，列出从克隆仓库到得到 system_top.xsa 所需依次执行的命令，并说明每条命令产出的中间产物。

lectures.title: 顶层 system_top 与 block design
lectures.topic: 以 zc706_fmcs2 为例，讲解 system_top.v 顶层 wrapper、system.bd block design 与 openwifi.tcl 顶层工程脚本如何组织整个 SoC 设计（DDR、PS、射频数据通路）。
learning_goals: 理解 system_top.v 作为 FPGA 顶层 wrapper 的结构
了解 system.bd block design 在 Vivado IP Integrator 中的角色
看懂 openwifi.tcl 如何创建并配置顶层工程
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 system_top.v 与 openwifi.tcl，列出顶层工程的顶层模块名、目标器件（part）来源，并指出约束文件 system.xdc 的作用。

lectures.title: openwifi_ip 层级：六大 IP 如何拼接
lectures.topic: 讲解 ip/openwifi_ip.tcl 如何把 xpu、tx_intf、rx_intf、openofdm_tx、openofdm_rx、side_ch 六个 IP 实例化为一个 openwifi_ip 层级子设计，并说明它们之间 I/Q、控制与状态信号的互连关系。
learning_goals: 理解 openwifi_ip 层级包含的六个核心 IP
看清各 IP 之间 I/Q、PHY 控制、CSI 等信号的流向
掌握 block design 层级复用（write_bd_tcl/source）思路
lectures.practice_task: 依据 openwifi_ip.tcl 绘制六个 IP 之间的连接示意图，标出发射链路（tx_intf→openofdm_tx→DAC）与接收链路（ADC→rx_intf→openofdm_rx）的数据方向。

lectures.title: PS-PL 互连：AXI、DMA 与中断
lectures.topic: 讲解 connect_openwifi_ip.tcl 如何把 openwifi_ip 接入 Zynq PS：AXI4-Lite 寄存器（S00_AXI/M_AXI_GP1）、AXI DMA（S_AXI_ACP/S_AXI_HP3 收发数据）、以及 tx_itrpt/rx_pkt_intr 等中断信号接到 sys_concat_intc。
learning_goals: 理解 PS 与 PL 之间三类通路：寄存器、DMA 数据、中断
看懂 AXI ACP/HP 端口在收发包 DMA 中的作用
掌握中断拼接器 sys_concat_intc 的接线方式
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 connect_openwifi_ip.tcl，列出 openwifi_ip 暴露给 PS 的 AXI 接口与中断输出，并说明收发数据分别走哪个 PS 端口（ACP/HP3）。

lectures.title: 板级配置与时钟体系
lectures.topic: 讲解 board_def.v 与构建期生成的 clock_speed.v 中的关键宏：NUM_CLK_PER_US、SAMPLING_RATE_MHZ(20)、NUM_CLK_PER_SAMPLE、COUNT_SCALE，以及 SMALL_FPGA 等板级规模开关如何影响参数化。
learning_goals: 理解基带时钟频率 NUM_CLK_PER_US 的含义与可选值
掌握采样率与每采样时钟数的换算关系
了解 SMALL_FPGA 等宏如何缩小缓冲深度以适配小容量器件
lectures.practice_task: 在 board_def.v 与 ip_repo_gen.tcl 中找到 NUM_CLK_PER_US、SAMPLING_RATE_MHZ 的定义，计算 NUM_CLK_PER_SAMPLE 的值，并解释切换到 240MHz(zcu102) 时需改动哪里。

lectures.title: rx_intf 接收接口总览
lectures.topic: 讲解 rx_intf.v 顶层模块：从 AD9361 ADC 读取 I/Q 样点，与 openofdm_rx 交互包/字节流，做 FCS 校验，并通过 AXI-Stream/AXI DMA 把有效帧送到 PS，同时支持发射时静音自收。
learning_goals: 理解 rx_intf 在接收链路中的位置与职责
看懂 ADC 样点输入与到 openofdm_rx 的 sample 接口
了解 mute_adc_out_to_bb、block_rx_dma_to_ps 等控制信号作用
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 rx_intf.v 的端口列表，整理出三类接口：ADC 数据输入、与 openofdm_rx 的样点/包信号、与 xpu 的控制信号，并说明 rx_pkt_intr 何时触发。

lectures.title: rx_intf 关键子模块
lectures.topic: 深入 rx_intf 的子模块：adc_intf（ADC 接口/重采样）、rx_iq_intf（I/Q 通路与增益）、rx_intf_m_axis/rx_intf_pl_to_m_axis（向 DMA 输出 AXI-Stream）、byte_to_word_fcs_sn_insert（字节拼字与 FCS/序号插入）。
learning_goals: 理解 ADC 接口与 I/Q 接口子模块的职责
看懂接收数据如何被打包成 AXI-Stream 送往 DMA
了解 FCS 校验结果与序号如何插入接收帧
lectures.practice_task: 选择 byte_to_word_fcs_sn_insert.v，说明它如何把 openofdm_rx 输出的字节流拼成 64bit AXI-Stream 字，并如何附加 FCS 结果与序号到 DMA 头部。

lectures.title: openofdm_rx：OFDM 接收机（外部子模块）
lectures.topic: 介绍作为 git 子模块引入的 openofdm_rx（来自 openofdm 项目 dot11zynq 分支），它完成包检测、同步、FFT、信道均衡与 Viterbi 译码；说明 get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh 如何把它放入 ip/openofdm_rx 并参与打包。
learning_goals: 理解 openofdm_rx 在接收链路完成的物理层处理
了解 openofdm_rx 作为子模块的引入与集成方式
认识包检测、同步、均衡、Viterbi 等关键步骤的分工
lectures.practice_task: 运行/阅读 get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh，说明 openofdm_rx 的来源仓库与分支，并解释 ip_repo_gen.tcl 在打包 openofdm_rx 时与其余 IP 的差异（pre_def 追加方式）。

lectures.title: openofdm_tx OFDM 发射机总览
lectures.topic: 讲解 openofdm_tx.v 顶层与 dot11_tx.v：接收 phy_tx_start，从 BRAM 读取待发字节，按 802.11 a/g/n 各 MCS 生成基带 I/Q（result_i/result_q），完成后给出 phy_tx_done/phy_tx_started。
learning_goals: 理解 openofdm_tx 在发射链路中的位置与触发流程
看懂 BRAM 输入与 I/Q 输出接口
了解 phy_tx_start/started/done 的握手时序
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 openofdm_tx.v 与 dot11_tx.v 的端口，画出从 phy_tx_start 到 result_iq_valid 的处理时序，并说明 bram_din/bram_addr 如何被驱动。

lectures.title: 发射信号处理子模块（编码/交织/IFFT/调制/前导）
lectures.topic: 深入 dot11_tx 的内部信号处理链：卷积编码(convenc)、打孔与交织查表(punc_interlv_lut)、调制映射(modulation)、IFFT(ifftmain 及 butterfly/ifftstage)、前导与训练序列 ROM(l_stf/l_ltf/ht_stf/ht_ltf_rom)、加扰与 CRC32。
learning_goals: 理解 802.11 发射基带各处理阶段的顺序
看懂卷积编码/打孔/交织的实现与查表
了解 IFFT 与前导 ROM 如何生成 OFDM 符号
lectures.practice_task: 在 dot11_tx.v 中追踪一个待发字节到 I/Q 样点的处理路径，列出依次经过的子模块（编码→打孔/交织→调制→IFFT→前导拼接）。

lectures.title: tx_intf 发射接口与 BRAM 缓存
lectures.topic: 讲解 tx_intf.v：从 AXI DMA 接收待发数据写入 BRAM，触发 openofdm_tx 生成 I/Q，再经 tx_iq_intf/tx_bit_intf 与 dac_intf 送往 AD9361 DAC；并介绍 tx_status_fifo、ht_sig_crc_calc、csi_fuzzer 等子模块。
learning_goals: 理解 tx_intf 在发射链路的缓存与调度职责
看懂 DMA→BRAM→openofdm_tx→DAC 的数据流
了解 HT-SIG CRC、TX 状态 FIFO、CSI fuzzer 的作用
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 tx_intf.v，说明一份待发数据如何从 AXI-Stream DMA 进入 BRAM、再被 openofdm_tx 消费并最终送到 dac_data，并指出 tx_status 的用途。

units.title: 控制核心 xpu 与低层 MAC

lectures.title: xpu 控制核心总览
lectures.topic: 讲解 xpu.v（eXternal Processing Unit）作为整个 WiFi 低层 MAC 与控制中枢：协调 CSMA/CA、TX 控制/重传、TSF 定时、接收包解析与地址过滤、RSSI/CCA、对 AD9361 的 SPI 控制以及队列 slice_en。
learning_goals: 理解 xpu 作为低层 MAC 控制中枢的职责
看懂 xpu 与 rx_intf/openofdm_rx/tx_intf/side_ch 之间的信号
了解 slice_en 队列门控与各类 LED/GPIO 状态输出
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 xpu.v 的端口，按“到 rx_intf / 到 openofdm_rx / 到 tx_intf / 到 side_ch / 到 PS”分组整理其对外信号，并说明 retrans_in_progress、backoff_done 的含义。

lectures.title: CSMA/CA 信道接入
lectures.topic: 深入 csma_ca.v：基于 TSF 1us 脉冲实现 DIFS/EIFS/SIFS/Slot 计时、随机退避（CW/slot）、NAV 虚拟载波监听，结合 tx_status 与 RSSI 决定何时允许发射。
learning_goals: 理解 802.11 DCF 的 DIFS/SIFS/Slot/退避机制
看懂 csma_ca 如何用 TSF 脉冲实现亚微秒级计时
了解 NAV、CW、退避计数器在源码中的实现
lectures.practice_task: 在 csma_ca.v 中定位 DIFS/SIFS/slot_time 等参数的来源，并追踪随机退避计数器 num_slot_random/cw 如何影响 backoff_done 的产生。

lectures.title: TX 控制、重传与 ACK
lectures.topic: 深入 tx_control.v：管理发射状态机（tx_control_state），处理 SIFS 后的 ACK 发送、ACK 超时、重传计数（max_num_retrans/tx_pkt_retrans_limit）、聚合(tx_ht_aggr)与 quit_retrans/reset_backoff。
learning_goals: 理解 TX 发射状态机的状态与跳转
看懂 ACK 等待、超时与重传次数控制
了解聚合帧与重传终止条件的处理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 tx_control.v，列出 tx_control_state 的关键状态及其含义，并说明收到 ACK 与 ACK 超时两种情况下状态机如何分别处理。

lectures.title: TSF 定时器与接收包解析/过滤
lectures.topic: 讲解 tsf_timer.v（802.11 64bit 同步定时器与 1us 脉冲）、phy_rx_parse.v（解析接收帧的 FC/addr1/addr2/addr3/SC）与 pkt_filter_ctl.v（地址过滤、控制 block_rx_dma_to_ps），实现按地址过滤与 ACK/重传所需的信息提取。
learning_goals: 理解 64bit TSF 定时器的实现与 1us 脉冲的作用
看懂接收帧 MAC 头部字段（FC/地址/序号）的解析
了解地址过滤如何控制 RX DMA 是否上报 PS
lectures.practice_task: 依据 phy_rx_parse.v 说明它从 ofdm_byte 流中提取哪些 MAC 字段，并结合 pkt_filter_ctl.v 解释地址过滤如何决定 block_rx_dma_to_ps 的取值。

lectures.title: CCA、RSSI 与 AD9361 SPI 控制
lectures.topic: 讲解 xpu 内的能量/信道检测链：rssi.v、iq_abs_avg.v、iq_rssi_to_db.v、mv_avg.v/mv_avg_dual_ch.v、dc_rm.v，以及 cca.v 信道空闲评估；并介绍 spi.v 如何经 side/SPI 控制寄存器配置 AD9361。
learning_goals: 理解 RSSI 从 I/Q 到 dB 的计算与平滑链路
看懂 CCA 信道空闲评估的实现
了解 xpu 如何通过 SPI 控制射频前端 AD9361
lectures.practice_task: 追踪 RSSI 的计算路径：从 ddc_i/ddc_q 经 mv_avg/iq_abs_avg/iq_rssi_to_db 到 rssi_half_db，并说明 cca.v 如何利用能量判断信道空闲。

lectures.title: side_ch 侧信道监控（CSI/RSSI/IQ 捕获与计数）
lectures.topic: 讲解 side_ch.v：作为研究/调试用侧信道，捕获发射/接收 I/Q、CSI（ofdm_symbol_eq_out）、RSSI、前导检测、demod 状态等，经 side_ch_counter_event_cfg 触发计数，由 side_ch_m_axis 经 DMA 上报 PS。
learning_goals: 理解 side_ch 用于可观测性与 CSI 采集的定位
看懂侧信道捕获的各类信号来源（TX/RX IQ、CSI、RSSI）
了解计数事件配置与经 DMA 上报的通路
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 side_ch.v，列出它能捕获的信号来源，并说明 side_ch_counter_event_cfg.v 如何配置触发计数、side_ch_m_axis.v 如何把结果送到 DMA。

units.title: 进阶开发与二次开发实践

lectures.title: AXI 寄存器映射与软件交互
lectures.topic: 讲解各 IP 的 *_s_axi.v（以 xpu_s_axi.v 为例）实现的 AXI4-Lite 寄存器组：slv_reg0..N 的读写地址映射，软件（openwifi 驱动）如何经这些寄存器配置参数、读取状态并控制 IP 行为。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI4-Lite 从设备寄存器组的实现模式
看懂寄存器地址到 slv_reg 的映射
了解驱动软件如何通过寄存器与 FPGA IP 交互
lectures.practice_task: 以 xpu_s_axi.v 为例，列出其 slv_reg 的数量与各自地址偏移，并说明软件读写某个配置寄存器时对应的 AXI 握手过程。

lectures.title: 条件编译与 Verilog 宏体系
lectures.topic: 讲解 _pre_def.v 条件编译机制：create_ip_repo.sh / create_vivado_proj.sh 把 BOARD_NAME、NUM_CLK_PER_US 及用户 DEF 转成 `define IP_NAME_DEF，以及 SMALL_FPGA、HAS_SIDE_CH、SIDE_CH_LESS_BRAM、ENABLE_DBG 等宏如何启用/裁剪代码块。
learning_goals: 理解 _pre_def.v 的生成与条件编译机制
掌握通过命令行参数向 Verilog 注入宏的方法
了解各类功能裁剪/调试宏的作用
lectures.practice_task: 依据 README 与脚本，写出一条为所有 IP 启用 ILA/DEBUG 宏的 create_ip_repo.sh 命令，并说明对应 `define 会写入哪个文件。

lectures.title: IP 仿真与 testbench 实践
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用 create_vivado_proj.sh 创建单 IP 工程并在 XSim 中跑 testbench：以 mv_avg_tb.v、fifo_sample_delay_tb.v、adc_intf_tb.v、dot11_tx_tb.v 为例，介绍 $fopen/$fscanf/$fwrite 读写测试向量、*_tb.tcl 配置与波形观测。
learning_goals: 掌握创建单 IP 工程并运行仿真的流程
看懂 testbench 用文件 IO 读写测试向量的方法
了解各 IP 提供的 testbench 与测试向量组织
lectures.practice_task: 选择 mv_avg_tb.v（或 dot11_tx_tb.v），说明它如何从 test_vec 读取输入、驱动 DUT 并写回结果，并描述在 Vivado 中启动行为级仿真的步骤。

lectures.title: 修改并打包自定义 IP
lectures.topic: 讲解“修改 IP 核心”工作流：在 ip/<ip_name> 下用 create_vivado_proj.sh 创建/编辑 IP 工程，再通过 package_ip.tcl / package_ip_complex.tcl 打包，最后用 create_ip_repo.sh 重新集成进顶层 openwifi 工程。
learning_goals: 掌握从修改单个 IP 到重新集成顶层的完整流程
看懂 package_ip.tcl / package_ip_complex.tcl 的打包逻辑
了解 ip_repo_gen.tcl 如何把各 IP 组装进 ip_repo
lectures.practice_task: 选择一个 IP（如 tx_intf），描述修改其源码后需要依次运行的脚本，并说明 package_ip_complex.tcl 与 package_ip.tcl 在打包参数上的差异。

lectures.title: 板卡移植与 ADI/Vivado 升级
lectures.topic: 讲解把 openwifi 迁移到新板卡或新 ADI/Vivado 版本的两种方法（Vivado 自动升级导出 .tcl 对比；write_bd_tcl 导出 openwifi_ip 层级再 source 进新 ADI 参考），并介绍 parse_board_name.tcl、ultra_scale_tcl_gen.sh、openwifi_ip_ultra_scale.tcl 的板级/器件适配作用。
learning_goals: 掌握把 openwifi_ip 层级迁移到新设计的思路
看懂 parse_board_name.tcl 的板名解析与 fpga_size_flag 作用
了解 UltraScale+ 与普通 Zynq 两套 tcl 的差异
lectures.practice_task: 依据 README 的 Migrate 小节与 parse_board_name.tcl，说明如何把 openwifi_ip 层级迁移到新的 ADI 参考设计，并指出 fpga_size_flag 如何影响 SMALL_FPGA 与 IP 列表。

lectures.title: GPIO/LED 调试、ILA 与 ENABLE_DBG
lectures.topic: 讲解硬件级调试手段：gpio_led.md 中各板卡 LED/GPIO 到 FPGA 信号（cycle_start/sig_valid/phy_tx_started/fcs_ok 等）的映射、flip/raw 两种风格，以及通过 ENABLE_DBG 宏插入 mark_debug、配合 ILA(.ltx) 抓波形的方法。
learning_goals: 理解 LED/GPIO 如何映射到关键 FPGA 状态信号
掌握 flip/raw 两种指示风格
学会用 ENABLE_DBG + ILA 进行片上波形调试
lectures.practice_task: 依据 gpio_led.md 选定一块板卡，列出至少 3 个 LED/GPIO 对应的 FPGA 信号及其含义，并说明如何用 ENABLE_DBG 宏在 openwifi.tcl/create_ip_repo.sh 中启用 ILA 抓取这些信号。

data.head: d047d794195beb72e12d2a9a6c205c16399cf288
data.rationale: openwifi-hw 是一个基于 Xilinx Zynq SoC + Analog Devices AD9361/9364 射频前端的 IEEE 802.11 a/b/g/n 全栈 SDR Wi-Fi FPGA 设计，构建在 ADI HDL 参考设计之上。学习顺序按“先看清全貌、再沿数据链路深入、最后做二次开发”设计：u1 让读者从零建立项目定位、目录、板卡与构建流程的整体认知；u2 进入顶层 block design 与 PS-PL 互连，理解 6 个自定义 IP（xpu/tx_intf/rx_intf/openofdm_tx/openofdm_rx/side_ch）如何拼接成 openwifi_ip 层级并与 PS 通信；u3-u4 沿接收与发射两条物理层数据链路逐模块读源码；u5 聚焦控制核心 xpu 与低层 MAC（CSMA/CA、重传/ACK、TSF、包解析过滤、CCA/RSSI/SPI）；u6 讲可观测性的 side_ch；u7 面向专家层的二次开发（寄存器映射、条件编译宏、仿真 testbench、自定义 IP 打包、板卡移植、GPIO/ILA 调试）。每篇讲义只解决一个主题并绑定真实源码与实践任务，依赖关系沿“总览→链路→控制→开发”递进。所有讲义均为新增（action=new）。

lectures.title: 项目总览：什么是 openwifi 与 SDR Wi-Fi
lectures.topic: 介绍 openwifi 项目定位：Linux mac80211 兼容的 SDR 全栈 Wi-Fi 设计，说明 openwifi-hw（FPGA 硬件）与 openwifi（驱动/软件）仓库的关系，以及 AGPLv3 与订阅双授权模式。
learning_goals: 理解 openwifi 是基于 SDR 的 IEEE 802.11 全栈实现
区分 openwifi-hw 硬件仓库与 openwifi 软件仓库的职责
了解项目授权与第三方模块（ADI HDL、openofdm）来源
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README.md，用自己的话写出 openwifi-hw 在整套 openwifi 系统中承担的角色，并列举它依赖的两个第三方模块（adi-hdl、openofdm）的作用。

lectures.title: 目录结构、子模块与软硬件边界
lectures.topic: 讲解仓库顶层目录布局：ip/（自定义 WiFi IP 核心）、boards/（板卡工程）、adi-hdl（ADI 参考设计子模块）、ip/openofdm_rx（openofdm 子模块），以及 .gitmodules 子模块配置。
learning_goals: 掌握仓库主要目录的功能划分
理解 adi-hdl 与 openofdm_rx 两个 git 子模块的作用
区分 FPGA 侧（PL）与 ARM 侧（PS）的代码边界
lectures.practice_task: 画出仓库的目录树，标注 ip/ 下 6 个自定义 IP 核心目录，并用 git submodule status（或阅读 .gitmodules）确认 adi-hdl 与 openofdm_rx 两个子模块。

lectures.title: 支持的板卡与开发运行环境
lectures.topic: 介绍 README 中列出的全部 BOARD_NAME 板卡（zc706/zed/zc702/adrv9361z7035/adrv9364z7020/zcu102/antsdr/e310v2/sdrpi 等）及其 Vivado 许可需求，以及 Vivado 2022.2 + Vitis、libtinfo5 等运行前置条件。
learning_goals: 了解 openwifi-hw 支持的硬件板卡与射频前端组合
理解 BOARD_NAME 环境变量如何驱动构建
掌握 Vivado/Vitis 版本与系统依赖要求
lectures.practice_task: 在 README 的板卡表中任选一块板卡，整理出它对应的 FPGA 芯片、射频前端、是否需要 Vivado 许可，并列出构建该板卡所需的环境变量。

lectures.title: FPGA 构建全流程实战（脚本链路）
lectures.topic: 从 prepare_adi_lib.sh、prepare_adi_board_ip.sh、get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh，到 boards/create_ip_repo.sh → ip_repo_gen.tcl → openwifi.tcl，再到 sdk_update.sh，梳理完整的“准备依赖-生成 IP 仓-建顶层工程-导出镜像”脚本链路。
learning_goals: 掌握 openwifi-hw 从零到 bitstream 的完整脚本流程
理解 create_ip_repo.sh 与 openwifi.tcl 各自的职责
了解 .xsa/.ltx 镜像如何导出供软件仓库使用
lectures.practice_task: 按照 README 的 Build FPGA 小节，列出从克隆仓库到得到 system_top.xsa 所需依次执行的命令，并说明每条命令产出的中间产物。

lectures.title: 顶层 system_top 与 block design
lectures.topic: 以 zc706_fmcs2 为例，讲解 system_top.v 顶层 wrapper、system.bd block design 与 openwifi.tcl 顶层工程脚本如何组织整个 SoC 设计（DDR、PS、射频数据通路）。
learning_goals: 理解 system_top.v 作为 FPGA 顶层 wrapper 的结构
了解 system.bd block design 在 Vivado IP Integrator 中的角色
看懂 openwifi.tcl 如何创建并配置顶层工程
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 system_top.v 与 openwifi.tcl，列出顶层工程的顶层模块名、目标器件（part）来源，并指出约束文件 system.xdc 的作用。

lectures.title: openwifi_ip 层级：六大 IP 如何拼接
lectures.topic: 讲解 ip/openwifi_ip.tcl 如何把 xpu、tx_intf、rx_intf、openofdm_tx、openofdm_rx、side_ch 六个 IP 实例化为一个 openwifi_ip 层级子设计，并说明它们之间 I/Q、控制与状态信号的互连关系。
learning_goals: 理解 openwifi_ip 层级包含的六个核心 IP
看清各 IP 之间 I/Q、PHY 控制、CSI 等信号的流向
掌握 block design 层级复用（write_bd_tcl/source）思路
lectures.practice_task: 依据 openwifi_ip.tcl 绘制六个 IP 之间的连接示意图，标出发射链路（tx_intf→openofdm_tx→DAC）与接收链路（ADC→rx_intf→openofdm_rx）的数据方向。

lectures.title: PS-PL 互连：AXI、DMA 与中断
lectures.topic: 讲解 connect_openwifi_ip.tcl 如何把 openwifi_ip 接入 Zynq PS：AXI4-Lite 寄存器（S00_AXI/M_AXI_GP1）、AXI DMA（S_AXI_ACP/S_AXI_HP3 收发数据）、以及 tx_itrpt/rx_pkt_intr 等中断信号接到 sys_concat_intc。
learning_goals: 理解 PS 与 PL 之间三类通路：寄存器、DMA 数据、中断
看懂 AXI ACP/HP 端口在收发包 DMA 中的作用
掌握中断拼接器 sys_concat_intc 的接线方式
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 connect_openwifi_ip.tcl，列出 openwifi_ip 暴露给 PS 的 AXI 接口与中断输出，并说明收发数据分别走哪个 PS 端口（ACP/HP3）。

lectures.title: 板级配置与时钟体系
lectures.topic: 讲解 board_def.v 与构建期生成的 clock_speed.v 中的关键宏：NUM_CLK_PER_US、SAMPLING_RATE_MHZ(20)、NUM_CLK_PER_SAMPLE、COUNT_SCALE，以及 SMALL_FPGA 等板级规模开关如何影响参数化。
learning_goals: 理解基带时钟频率 NUM_CLK_PER_US 的含义与可选值
掌握采样率与每采样时钟数的换算关系
了解 SMALL_FPGA 等宏如何缩小缓冲深度以适配小容量器件
lectures.practice_task: 在 board_def.v 与 ip_repo_gen.tcl 中找到 NUM_CLK_PER_US、SAMPLING_RATE_MHZ 的定义，计算 NUM_CLK_PER_SAMPLE 的值，并解释切换到 240MHz(zcu102) 时需改动哪里。

lectures.title: rx_intf 接收接口总览
lectures.topic: 讲解 rx_intf.v 顶层模块：从 AD9361 ADC 读取 I/Q 样点，与 openofdm_rx 交互包/字节流，做 FCS 校验，并通过 AXI-Stream/AXI DMA 把有效帧送到 PS，同时支持发射时静音自收。
learning_goals: 理解 rx_intf 在接收链路中的位置与职责
看懂 ADC 样点输入与到 openofdm_rx 的 sample 接口
了解 mute_adc_out_to_bb、block_rx_dma_to_ps 等控制信号作用
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 rx_intf.v 的端口列表，整理出三类接口：ADC 数据输入、与 openofdm_rx 的样点/包信号、与 xpu 的控制信号，并说明 rx_pkt_intr 何时触发。

lectures.title: rx_intf 关键子模块
lectures.topic: 深入 rx_intf 的子模块：adc_intf（ADC 接口/重采样）、rx_iq_intf（I/Q 通路与增益）、rx_intf_m_axis/rx_intf_pl_to_m_axis（向 DMA 输出 AXI-Stream）、byte_to_word_fcs_sn_insert（字节拼字与 FCS/序号插入）。
learning_goals: 理解 ADC 接口与 I/Q 接口子模块的职责
看懂接收数据如何被打包成 AXI-Stream 送往 DMA
了解 FCS 校验结果与序号如何插入接收帧
lectures.practice_task: 选择 byte_to_word_fcs_sn_insert.v，说明它如何把 openofdm_rx 输出的字节流拼成 64bit AXI-Stream 字，并如何附加 FCS 结果与序号到 DMA 头部。

lectures.title: openofdm_rx：OFDM 接收机（外部子模块）
lectures.topic: 介绍作为 git 子模块引入的 openofdm_rx（来自 openofdm 项目 dot11zynq 分支），它完成包检测、同步、FFT、信道均衡与 Viterbi 译码；说明 get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh 如何把它放入 ip/openofdm_rx 并参与打包。
learning_goals: 理解 openofdm_rx 在接收链路完成的物理层处理
了解 openofdm_rx 作为子模块的引入与集成方式
认识包检测、同步、均衡、Viterbi 等关键步骤的分工
lectures.practice_task: 运行/阅读 get_ip_openofdm_rx.sh，说明 openofdm_rx 的来源仓库与分支，并解释 ip_repo_gen.tcl 在打包 openofdm_rx 时与其余 IP 的差异（pre_def 追加方式）。

lectures.title: openofdm_tx OFDM 发射机总览
lectures.topic: 讲解 openofdm_tx.v 顶层与 dot11_tx.v：接收 phy_tx_start，从 BRAM 读取待发字节，按 802.11 a/g/n 各 MCS 生成基带 I/Q（result_i/result_q），完成后给出 phy_tx_done/phy_tx_started。
learning_goals: 理解 openofdm_tx 在发射链路中的位置与触发流程
看懂 BRAM 输入与 I/Q 输出接口
了解 phy_tx_start/started/done 的握手时序
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 openofdm_tx.v 与 dot11_tx.v 的端口，画出从 phy_tx_start 到 result_iq_valid 的处理时序，并说明 bram_din/bram_addr 如何被驱动。

lectures.title: 发射信号处理子模块（编码/交织/IFFT/调制/前导）
lectures.topic: 深入 dot11_tx 的内部信号处理链：卷积编码(convenc)、打孔与交织查表(punc_interlv_lut)、调制映射(modulation)、IFFT(ifftmain 及 butterfly/ifftstage)、前导与训练序列 ROM(l_stf/l_ltf/ht_stf/ht_ltf_rom)、加扰与 CRC32。
learning_goals: 理解 802.11 发射基带各处理阶段的顺序
看懂卷积编码/打孔/交织的实现与查表
了解 IFFT 与前导 ROM 如何生成 OFDM 符号
lectures.practice_task: 在 dot11_tx.v 中追踪一个待发字节到 I/Q 样点的处理路径，列出依次经过的子模块（编码→打孔/交织→调制→IFFT→前导拼接）。

lectures.title: tx_intf 发射接口与 BRAM 缓存
lectures.topic: 讲解 tx_intf.v：从 AXI DMA 接收待发数据写入 BRAM，触发 openofdm_tx 生成 I/Q，再经 tx_iq_intf/tx_bit_intf 与 dac_intf 送往 AD9361 DAC；并介绍 tx_status_fifo、ht_sig_crc_calc、csi_fuzzer 等子模块。
learning_goals: 理解 tx_intf 在发射链路的缓存与调度职责
看懂 DMA→BRAM→openofdm_tx→DAC 的数据流
了解 HT-SIG CRC、TX 状态 FIFO、CSI fuzzer 的作用
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 tx_intf.v，说明一份待发数据如何从 AXI-Stream DMA 进入 BRAM、再被 openofdm_tx 消费并最终送到 dac_data，并指出 tx_status 的用途。

units.title: 控制核心 xpu 与低层 MAC

lectures.title: xpu 控制核心总览
lectures.topic: 讲解 xpu.v（eXternal Processing Unit）作为整个 WiFi 低层 MAC 与控制中枢：协调 CSMA/CA、TX 控制/重传、TSF 定时、接收包解析与地址过滤、RSSI/CCA、对 AD9361 的 SPI 控制以及队列 slice_en。
learning_goals: 理解 xpu 作为低层 MAC 控制中枢的职责
看懂 xpu 与 rx_intf/openofdm_rx/tx_intf/side_ch 之间的信号
了解 slice_en 队列门控与各类 LED/GPIO 状态输出
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 xpu.v 的端口，按“到 rx_intf / 到 openofdm_rx / 到 tx_intf / 到 side_ch / 到 PS”分组整理其对外信号，并说明 retrans_in_progress、backoff_done 的含义。

lectures.title: CSMA/CA 信道接入
lectures.topic: 深入 csma_ca.v：基于 TSF 1us 脉冲实现 DIFS/EIFS/SIFS/Slot 计时、随机退避（CW/slot）、NAV 虚拟载波监听，结合 tx_status 与 RSSI 决定何时允许发射。
learning_goals: 理解 802.11 DCF 的 DIFS/SIFS/Slot/退避机制
看懂 csma_ca 如何用 TSF 脉冲实现亚微秒级计时
了解 NAV、CW、退避计数器在源码中的实现
lectures.practice_task: 在 csma_ca.v 中定位 DIFS/SIFS/slot_time 等参数的来源，并追踪随机退避计数器 num_slot_random/cw 如何影响 backoff_done 的产生。

lectures.title: TX 控制、重传与 ACK
lectures.topic: 深入 tx_control.v：管理发射状态机（tx_control_state），处理 SIFS 后的 ACK 发送、ACK 超时、重传计数（max_num_retrans/tx_pkt_retrans_limit）、聚合(tx_ht_aggr)与 quit_retrans/reset_backoff。
learning_goals: 理解 TX 发射状态机的状态与跳转
看懂 ACK 等待、超时与重传次数控制
了解聚合帧与重传终止条件的处理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 tx_control.v，列出 tx_control_state 的关键状态及其含义，并说明收到 ACK 与 ACK 超时两种情况下状态机如何分别处理。

lectures.title: TSF 定时器与接收包解析/过滤
lectures.topic: 讲解 tsf_timer.v（802.11 64bit 同步定时器与 1us 脉冲）、phy_rx_parse.v（解析接收帧的 FC/addr1/addr2/addr3/SC）与 pkt_filter_ctl.v（地址过滤、控制 block_rx_dma_to_ps），实现按地址过滤与 ACK/重传所需的信息提取。
learning_goals: 理解 64bit TSF 定时器的实现与 1us 脉冲的作用
看懂接收帧 MAC 头部字段（FC/地址/序号）的解析
了解地址过滤如何控制 RX DMA 是否上报 PS
lectures.practice_task: 依据 phy_rx_parse.v 说明它从 ofdm_byte 流中提取哪些 MAC 字段，并结合 pkt_filter_ctl.v 解释地址过滤如何决定 block_rx_dma_to_ps 的取值。

lectures.title: CCA、RSSI 与 AD9361 SPI 控制
lectures.topic: 讲解 xpu 内的能量/信道检测链：rssi.v、iq_abs_avg.v、iq_rssi_to_db.v、mv_avg.v/mv_avg_dual_ch.v、dc_rm.v，以及 cca.v 信道空闲评估；并介绍 spi.v 如何经 side/SPI 控制寄存器配置 AD9361。
learning_goals: 理解 RSSI 从 I/Q 到 dB 的计算与平滑链路
看懂 CCA 信道空闲评估的实现
了解 xpu 如何通过 SPI 控制射频前端 AD9361
lectures.practice_task: 追踪 RSSI 的计算路径：从 ddc_i/ddc_q 经 mv_avg/iq_abs_avg/iq_rssi_to_db 到 rssi_half_db，并说明 cca.v 如何利用能量判断信道空闲。

lectures.title: side_ch 侧信道监控（CSI/RSSI/IQ 捕获与计数）
lectures.topic: 讲解 side_ch.v：作为研究/调试用侧信道，捕获发射/接收 I/Q、CSI（ofdm_symbol_eq_out）、RSSI、前导检测、demod 状态等，经 side_ch_counter_event_cfg 触发计数，由 side_ch_m_axis 经 DMA 上报 PS。
learning_goals: 理解 side_ch 用于可观测性与 CSI 采集的定位
看懂侧信道捕获的各类信号来源（TX/RX IQ、CSI、RSSI）
了解计数事件配置与经 DMA 上报的通路
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 side_ch.v，列出它能捕获的信号来源，并说明 side_ch_counter_event_cfg.v 如何配置触发计数、side_ch_m_axis.v 如何把结果送到 DMA。

units.title: 进阶开发与二次开发实践

lectures.title: AXI 寄存器映射与软件交互
lectures.topic: 讲解各 IP 的 *_s_axi.v（以 xpu_s_axi.v 为例）实现的 AXI4-Lite 寄存器组：slv_reg0..N 的读写地址映射，软件（openwifi 驱动）如何经这些寄存器配置参数、读取状态并控制 IP 行为。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI4-Lite 从设备寄存器组的实现模式
看懂寄存器地址到 slv_reg 的映射
了解驱动软件如何通过寄存器与 FPGA IP 交互
lectures.practice_task: 以 xpu_s_axi.v 为例，列出其 slv_reg 的数量与各自地址偏移，并说明软件读写某个配置寄存器时对应的 AXI 握手过程。

lectures.title: 条件编译与 Verilog 宏体系
lectures.topic: 讲解 _pre_def.v 条件编译机制：create_ip_repo.sh / create_vivado_proj.sh 把 BOARD_NAME、NUM_CLK_PER_US 及用户 DEF 转成 `define IP_NAME_DEF，以及 SMALL_FPGA、HAS_SIDE_CH、SIDE_CH_LESS_BRAM、ENABLE_DBG 等宏如何启用/裁剪代码块。
learning_goals: 理解 _pre_def.v 的生成与条件编译机制
掌握通过命令行参数向 Verilog 注入宏的方法
了解各类功能裁剪/调试宏的作用
lectures.practice_task: 依据 README 与脚本，写出一条为所有 IP 启用 ILA/DEBUG 宏的 create_ip_repo.sh 命令，并说明对应 `define 会写入哪个文件。

lectures.title: IP 仿真与 testbench 实践
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用 create_vivado_proj.sh 创建单 IP 工程并在 XSim 中跑 testbench：以 mv_avg_tb.v、fifo_sample_delay_tb.v、adc_intf_tb.v、dot11_tx_tb.v 为例，介绍 $fopen/$fscanf/$fwrite 读写测试向量、*_tb.tcl 配置与波形观测。
learning_goals: 掌握创建单 IP 工程并运行仿真的流程
看懂 testbench 用文件 IO 读写测试向量的方法
了解各 IP 提供的 testbench 与测试向量组织
lectures.practice_task: 选择 mv_avg_tb.v（或 dot11_tx_tb.v），说明它如何从 test_vec 读取输入、驱动 DUT 并写回结果，并描述在 Vivado 中启动行为级仿真的步骤。

lectures.title: 修改并打包自定义 IP
lectures.topic: 讲解“修改 IP 核心”工作流：在 ip/<ip_name> 下用 create_vivado_proj.sh 创建/编辑 IP 工程，再通过 package_ip.tcl / package_ip_complex.tcl 打包，最后用 create_ip_repo.sh 重新集成进顶层 openwifi 工程。
learning_goals: 掌握从修改单个 IP 到重新集成顶层的完整流程
看懂 package_ip.tcl / package_ip_complex.tcl 的打包逻辑
了解 ip_repo_gen.tcl 如何把各 IP 组装进 ip_repo
lectures.practice_task: 选择一个 IP（如 tx_intf），描述修改其源码后需要依次运行的脚本，并说明 package_ip_complex.tcl 与 package_ip.tcl 在打包参数上的差异。

lectures.title: 板卡移植与 ADI/Vivado 升级
lectures.topic: 讲解把 openwifi 迁移到新板卡或新 ADI/Vivado 版本的两种方法（Vivado 自动升级导出 .tcl 对比；write_bd_tcl 导出 openwifi_ip 层级再 source 进新 ADI 参考），并介绍 parse_board_name.tcl、ultra_scale_tcl_gen.sh、openwifi_ip_ultra_scale.tcl 的板级/器件适配作用。
learning_goals: 掌握把 openwifi_ip 层级迁移到新设计的思路
看懂 parse_board_name.tcl 的板名解析与 fpga_size_flag 作用
了解 UltraScale+ 与普通 Zynq 两套 tcl 的差异
lectures.practice_task: 依据 README 的 Migrate 小节与 parse_board_name.tcl，说明如何把 openwifi_ip 层级迁移到新的 ADI 参考设计，并指出 fpga_size_flag 如何影响 SMALL_FPGA 与 IP 列表。

lectures.title: GPIO/LED 调试、ILA 与 ENABLE_DBG
lectures.topic: 讲解硬件级调试手段：gpio_led.md 中各板卡 LED/GPIO 到 FPGA 信号（cycle_start/sig_valid/phy_tx_started/fcs_ok 等）的映射、flip/raw 两种风格，以及通过 ENABLE_DBG 宏插入 mark_debug、配合 ILA(.ltx) 抓波形的方法。
learning_goals: 理解 LED/GPIO 如何映射到关键 FPGA 状态信号
掌握 flip/raw 两种指示风格
学会用 ENABLE_DBG + ILA 进行片上波形调试
lectures.practice_task: 依据 gpio_led.md 选定一块板卡，列出至少 3 个 LED/GPIO 对应的 FPGA 信号及其含义，并说明如何用 ENABLE_DBG 宏在 openwifi.tcl/create_ip_repo.sh 中启用 ILA 抓取这些信号。
